pi_items.source: ice_road_logistics_request.txt generated by setup_task.sh explicitly specifies 'Analyst Role: Logistics Planner' and involves civil engineering constraints.
ice_road_logistics_request.txt explicitly references 'Heavy mining equipment across the Lena River ice roads in Siberia'
air.mon.ltm.nc contains the 'air' variable, and it is explicitly referenced in ice_road_logistics_request.txt
List exactly matches REQUIRED DELIVERABLES in ice_road_logistics_request.txt and verifier.py assertions
ice_road_logistics_request.txt defines the location coordinates as 62.5°N
ice_road_logistics_request.txt defines the location coordinates as 130.0°E
ice_road_logistics_request.txt specifies 'Threshold in Kelvin = 253.15 K', properly matching -20°C

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world NCEP Reanalysis climate data (air.mon.ltm.nc) sourced from NOAA PSL. Code execution reveals the 'air' variable is natively stored with 'units: degC', contradicting the task prompt which incorrectly states the dataset is natively in Kelvin. By querying the dataset at the exact requested coordinates for Yakutsk (Lat 62.5, Lon 130.0), the empirically verified minimum temperature occurs in January at roughly -32.4°C (240.7 K). With the safe operating threshold set to -20°C (253.15 K), the accurate subset of 'safe' months for logistics transit is verified to be November, December, January, and February.
